name: France
intro: France's project profession is expanding beyond its engineering heartland: the grands groupes of aerospace, energy and transport still run some of Europe's largest programmes, but Station F's startup ecosystem, banking transformation and public-sector digitisation now hire just as actively. International certification signals capability across both worlds — PMP is recognised in the CAC 40's globalised delivery organisations, Scrum credentials in the tech scene. Our instruction is in English, which itself mirrors the international programmes where certified professionals in France typically work. The live online weekend batch runs on the IST schedule, converting to morning-to-afternoon French hours with evenings untouched. Afterwards we support the exam application, provide full-length mock exams, and keep the trainer reachable for questions until you clear the exam.

faq.q: How much does certification training cost in France?
faq.a: The course fee is US$1,295 for PMP and US$695 for CSM, covering the full live online weekend workshop, study materials, mock exams and exam application support. PMI's PMP exam fee (US$425 member / US$675 non-member) is paid separately to PMI; the CSM exam is included in the course fee.

faq.q: Are the classes live or recorded?
faq.a: Every batch is taught live by an instructor over two weekend days — not pre-recorded videos. You can ask questions in real time, work through exercises with the cohort, and reach the trainer afterwards for doubts.

faq.q: Is the certification recognised by employers in France?
faq.a: Yes. PMP (from PMI) and CSM (from Scrum Alliance) are global credentials recognised identically everywhere, including by employers and recruiters across France.
